long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple sequential steps, reagents, or transformations.

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions requiring counting of molecules, ions, bonds, or specific numerical calculations.

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms, intermediates, or transition states.

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties (acidity, basicity, reactivity, etc.) and arranging in orders.

questions.question_text: The gram molar mass of CO2 is: a) 46 g b) 78 g c) 44 g d) 88 g
questions.solution_summary: Molar mass of CO2 = 12 + 2(16) = 44 g mol^-1.

questions.question_text: Due to the presence of electrons in the inner shells, the electron in the outer shell will not experience the full positive charge of the nucleus. This is known as: a) shielding of the outer shell electrons from the nucleus by the inner shell electrons b) charge stealing by inner shell electrons from the outer shell electrons c) shielding of the inner shell electrons from the nucleus by the outer shell electrons d) charge stealing by outer shell electrons from the inner shell electrons
questions.solution_summary: Shielding effect (or screening effect) is the reduction in the attractive force of the nucleus on outer electrons due to inner electrons.

questions.question_text: Which one is the correct unit for entropy? a) kJ mol b) KJ mol^-1 c) JK^-1 mol d) JK^-1 mol^-1
questions.solution_summary: Entropy (S) has SI unit J K^-1 mol^-1 (Joule per Kelvin per mole).

questions.question_text: The pair of ions having same electronic configuration is: a) Cr3+ Fe3+ b) Sc3+ Cr3+ c) Fe3+ Co3+ d) Fe3+ Mn2+
questions.solution_summary: Fe3+ (Z=26, d5) and Mn2+ (Z=25, d5) both have 3d5 configuration (isoelectronic).

questions.question_text: An isochoric process takes place at constant: a) temperature b) concentration c) volume d) pressure
questions.solution_summary: Isochoric process = constant volume (V = constant).

questions.question_text: The electronic configuration 1s2 2s2 2p1 belongs to: a) Lithium b) Boron c) Carbon d) Beryllium
questions.solution_summary: Boron has atomic number 5, configuration 1s2 2s2 2p1.

questions.question_text: The standard emf of a galvanic cell involving 3 moles of electrons in its redox reaction is 0.59 V. The equilibrium constant for the reaction of the cell is: a) 10^30 b) 10^25 c) 10^15 d) 10^20

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Nernst equation relation with equilibrium constant.

questions.question_text: The organic compound given below is one of the four options. Choose the most appropriate one. (image: heterocyclic ring) a) alicyclic compound b) aromatic heterocyclic compound c) aliphatic heterocyclic compound d) benzenoid compound
questions.solution_summary: The compound is an aliphatic heterocyclic compound (cyclic with hetero atom but not aromatic).

questions.question_text: The following reaction is: (CH3)3C-Br →[H2O] (CH3)3C-OH a) Elimination reaction b) Substitution reaction c) Free radical reaction d) Displacement reaction
questions.solution_summary: Br is replaced by OH (nucleophilic substitution). Tertiary halide undergoes SN1.

questions.question_text: The size of isoelectronic species: F-, Ne and Na+ is affected by: a) nuclear charge (Z) b) valence principal quantum number (n) c) none of the factors because their size is the same d) electron-electron interaction in the outer orbitals
questions.solution_summary: For isoelectronic species, size depends on nuclear charge. Higher Z → smaller size. Order: Na+ < Ne < F-.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Effect of nuclear charge on isoelectronic species.

questions.question_text: A reaction, A + B → C + D + q is found to have a positive entropy change. The reaction will be: a) spontaneous at all temperature b) nonspontaneous at all temperature c) spontaneous only at low temperature d) spontaneous only at high temperature
questions.solution_summary: ΔH = -q (exothermic, negative). ΔS = positive. ΔG = ΔH - TΔS will be negative at all temperatures (negative minus positive).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Gibbs free energy equation.

questions.question_text: The compound formed as a result of oxidation of ethyl benzene by KMnO4 is: a) benzophenone b) acetophenone c) benzyl alcohol d) benzoic acid
questions.solution_summary: Alkylbenzenes (including ethylbenzene) on oxidation with KMnO4 give benzoic acid, regardless of side chain length.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Free radicals are short lived and highly reactive. Reason (R): Free radicals are highly unstable. a) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A b) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A c) A is true but R is false d) A is false but R is true
questions.solution_summary: Both statements are true. Free radicals have unpaired electrons, making them unstable and reactive. However, instability is not the explanation for reactivity; the unpaired electron makes them seek to pair up.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Activating groups are electron donors. Reason (R): Nitroso group is activating group. a) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A b) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A c) A is true but R is false d) A is false but R is true
questions.solution_summary: Assertion is true: activating groups are electron donating. Reason is false: nitroso group (-NO) is deactivating (electron withdrawing).

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Matter waves consist of oscillating electric and magnetic fields. Reason (R): Matter waves require medium for propagation. a) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A b) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A c) A is true but R is false d) A is false but R is true
questions.solution_summary: Assertion is false: electromagnetic waves consist of oscillating electric and magnetic fields, not matter waves. Reason is true: matter waves don't require a medium (unlike mechanical waves).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of matter waves and electromagnetic waves.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Molecular mass of methane is 16.034 u. Reason (R): Molecular mass of methane, which contains one carbon atom and four hydrogen atoms. a) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A b) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A c) A is true but R is false d) A is false but R is true
questions.solution_summary: Both statements are true. Molecular mass is sum of atomic masses. However, the reason just states the composition, not the calculation.

questions.question_text: PCl5, PCl3 and Cl2 are at equilibrium at 500K in a closed container and their concentrations are 0.8×10^-3 mol L^-1, 1.2×10^-3 mol L^-1 and 1.2×10^-3 mol L^-1 respectively. Calculate Kc for PCl5(g) ⇌ PCl3(g) + Cl2(g). OR Neutral solutions have pH = 7 at 298K. A sample of pure water is found to have pH < 7. Does it mean that it is acidic? Explain.
questions.correct_answer: Kc = [PCl3][Cl2]/[PCl5] = (1.2×10^-3)(1.2×10^-3)/(0.8×10^-3) = 1.8×10^-3. OR: No, water is neutral at all temperatures; pH < 7 means temperature > 298K, but [H+] = [OH-].
questions.solution_summary: Application of Kc expression with given concentrations.

questions.question_text: Write the alkenes that give the following compounds on ozonolysis: (i) CH3COCH2CH3 + CH3CHO (ii) CH3COCH2CH2CH3 + HCHO + CH3CHO
questions.solution_summary: Combining carbonyl fragments to reconstruct alkene.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Reconstruction of alkene from ozonolysis products.

questions.question_text: How would you justify the presence of 18 elements in the 5th period of the Periodic Table?
questions.correct_answer: 5th period has n=5. Orbitals filled: 5s (2), 4d (10), 5p (6) = total 18 electrons/elements.
questions.solution_summary: Based on filling of 5s, 4d, and 5p orbitals.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Explanation of period length based on orbital filling.

questions.question_text: How many significant figures should be present in the answer of the following calculations? (i) 0.02856 × 298.15 × 0.112 (ii) 5 × 5.364 (iii) 0.0125 + 0.7864 + 0.0215
questions.correct_answer: (i) 3 significant figures (0.112 has 3). (ii) 4 significant figures (5.364 has 4). (iii) 4 decimal places.
questions.solution_summary: Rules: multiplication gives least significant figures; addition gives least decimal places.

questions.question_text: Write the complete symbol for the atom with the given atomic number (Z) and atomic mass (A): (i) Z=17, A=35 (ii) Z=92, A=233 (iii) Z=4, A=9
questions.solution_summary: Symbol: ^A_Z X where X is element symbol.

questions.question_text: Although both CO2 and H2O are triatomic molecules, the shape of H2O molecule is bent while that of CO2 is linear. Explain this on the basis of dipole moment.
questions.correct_answer: CO2 has zero dipole moment (linear, bond dipoles cancel). H2O has net dipole moment (1.84 D), so bent shape (bond dipoles don't cancel).
questions.solution_summary: Dipole moment determines shape: zero dipole means linear; non-zero means bent.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Relationship between dipole moment and molecular shape.

questions.question_text: Answer: (a) A sample of 1.0 mole of a monoatomic ideal gas is taken through a cyclic process of expansion and compression. What will be the value of ΔH for the cycle as a whole? (b) What is an open system? (c) Write the mathematical expression of heat capacity.
questions.sub_topic: Cyclic Process, Open System, Heat Capacity
questions.correct_answer: (a) ΔH = 0 (state function, initial = final). (b) Open system: exchanges both matter and energy with surroundings. (c) q = c × m × ΔT.
questions.solution_summary: Definitions and concepts.

questions.question_text: Give main differences between a reversible process and an irreversible process.
questions.correct_answer: Reversible: infinite time, equilibrium at each step, maximum work, can be reversed. Irreversible: finite time, not at equilibrium, less work, cannot be reversed without permanent change.
questions.solution_summary: Key differences between reversible and irreversible processes.

questions.question_text: What are the oxidation numbers of the underlined element in KI3 and how do you rationalise your results?
questions.correct_answer: In KI3, average oxidation number of I is -1/3. But actual: K+ [I-I←I]-. One I is -1, two I are 0 (in I2).
questions.solution_summary: Average oxidation number is fractional but actual atoms have integer oxidation states.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of fractional oxidation states in polyiodides.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum number of emission lines when the excited electron of a atom in n=6 drops to the ground state?
questions.correct_answer: Maximum number of emission lines = n(n-1)/2 = 6×5/2 = 15.
questions.solution_summary: Number of possible transitions from n=6 to ground state.

questions.question_text: What is the significance of the terms - isolated gaseous atom and ground state while defining the ionization enthalpy and electron gain enthalpy?
questions.sub_topic: Ionisation Enthalpy and Electron Gain Enthalpy
questions.correct_answer: Isolated gaseous atom: no interatomic interactions, so only intrinsic properties. Ground state: lowest energy state, ensures standardisation.
questions.solution_summary: Significance of terms in definitions.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of standard conditions for enthalpy measurements.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between molality and molarity?
questions.correct_answer: Molarity = moles solute / volume solution (L), temperature dependent. Molality = moles solute / mass solvent (kg), temperature independent.
questions.solution_summary: Comparison of concentration units.

questions.question_text: Case: Classification of Organic Compounds. (i) The successive members of a homologous series differ by which mass of amu? (ii) Are pyridine, pyrrole, thiophene all heteroaromatic compounds? (iii) Difference between heterocyclic and homocyclic compound. OR: Is tetrahydrofuran aromatic?
questions.correct_answer: (i) 14 amu (-CH2). (ii) Yes. (iii) Heterocyclic: ring has hetero atom; homocyclic: all ring atoms same. OR: Tetrahydrofuran is non-aromatic (no conjugation, doesn't follow Huckel).
questions.solution_summary: Definitions and classification.

questions.question_text: Case: Dipole Moment. (i) Out of Cl2 and CO2 which compounds have zero dipole moments? (ii) A diatomic molecule has a dipole moment of 1.2D. If bond length is 1.0×10^-8 cm, what fraction of charge exists on each atom? (iii) Dipole moment of NF3 is much less than NH3. Why? OR: A covalent molecule x-y is found to have dipole moment 1.5×10^-29 cm and bond length 150 pm. What will be percentage of ionic character?
questions.correct_answer: (i) Both Cl2 and CO2 have zero dipole moment. (ii) Fraction = 1.2×10^-10 / 4.8×10^-10 = 0.25. (iii) In NF3, dipole moments of N-F bonds partially cancel due to direction; in NH3, N-H bond dipoles add. OR: % ionic = (1.5×10^-29 / 2.4×10^-29) × 100 = 62.5%.
questions.solution_summary: Dipole moment concepts and calculations.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of dipole moment concepts and calculations.

questions.question_text: Attempt any five: (a) What are cycloalkanes? (b) Classify hydrocarbons according to carbon-carbon bond. (c) Suggest a route for the preparation of nitrobenzene starting from acetylene. (d) Which conformation of ethane is more stable? (e) Why is iodination of benzene carried out in presence of nitric acid or iodic acid? (f) Methane does not react with chlorine in dark. Why? (g) Draw Newman's projection formula of staggered form of 1,2-dichloroethane.
questions.correct_answer: (a) Cyclic alkanes. (b) Saturated, unsaturated, aromatic. (c) Acetylene → benzene (cyclic polymerisation) → nitration → nitrobenzene. (d) Staggered. (e) HNO3/HIO3 oxidises HI to I2 preventing backward reaction. (f) No free radicals in dark. (g) Staggered Newman projection with Cl at 60°.
questions.solution_summary: Multiple organic chemistry concepts.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of various organic chemistry concepts.

questions.question_text: The ionization constant of HF is 3.2×10^-4. Calculate the degree of dissociation of HF in its 0.02 M solution. Calculate the concentration of all species present (H3O+, F- and HF) in the solution and its pH. OR Determine the solubilities of silver chromate, barium chromate, ferric hydroxide at 298K from their solubility product constants. (Ksp values given)
questions.sub_topic: Degree of Dissociation, pH, Solubility Product
questions.solution_summary: Acid dissociation equilibrium and Ksp calculations.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of acid dissociation and Ksp calculations.

questions.question_text: Answer: (a) (i) Describe the method to separate two compounds with different solubilities. (ii) Expand bond line formula to show all atoms. (b) (i) Which of CH3COOH and CH3COO- is more stabilised by resonance? Explain. (ii) An organic compound contains 69% carbon and 4.8% hydrogen. Calculate masses of CO2 and H2O produced when 0.20g of compound is combusted.
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Basic Concepts / Physical Chemistry - Stoichiometry
questions.correct_answer: (a)(i) Fractional crystallisation. (ii) Bond line expansion. (b)(i) CH3COO- is more stabilised (two equivalent resonance structures). (ii) Mass CO2 = 0.506 g, Mass H2O = 0.0864 g.
questions.solution_summary: Purification, resonance stabilisation, and combustion analysis.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multiple concepts: purification, resonance, and stoichiometric calculations.
